advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Dietary carbohydrate quality and quantity are the primary nutritional levers for blood glucose management in type 1 and type 2 diabetes. Glycemic index and glycemic load frameworks must be applied alongside total carbohydrate counting.
research_findings: Low-carbohydrate diets (20-50g/day) produce the greatest short-term improvements in HbA1c levels. Mediterranean and DASH dietary patterns show sustained HbA1c reductions of 0.3-0.8% over 12 months.
current_focus_areas: Developing personalized carbohydrate management protocols for T1D and T2D; building glycemic index food databases; researching meal timing and insulin sensitivity optimization strategies.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 9 (Sports Nutrition). Progress: 58%.
Progress update: Completed glycemic management protocols for T1D and T2D populations. Validated carbohydrate counting and glycemic index application frameworks. Progress advanced to 63%.
Phase transition: advanced from 'independent_learning' to 'collaborative_learning'. Bot 14 now entering collaborative learning phase. Progress: 72%.
